title: #pragma peephole off
description: Defeats branchless-ternary / branch-fusion peephole on a single function.

title: Narrow K&R extern to real signature
description: extern void fn() -> extern u32 fn(u32,s32) fixes param-move/clrlslwi instruction choice.

title: Per-function #pragma optimization_level
description: In-body opt-level override matches functions compiled at a different level than the TU.

title: Comma operator progressive index
description: (0,expr) / (iVar=N, key!=tbl[N]) chain produces sequential li rN,i for small-table lookup.

title: Early null block with valid label
description: For small index guards, use peephole off plus labels so negative branches fall through to a shared null return and the unsigned range check branches to the valid block.

title: No-temp RMW defeats W1/W2 base-register color
description: Multi-load+store 'shuffle' block (p[i]=p[j]; p[k]+=1; p[x]=0) where CW colors the base pointer wrong (too-low reg) and/or loads in descending offset order: write it as DIRECT read-modify-write with NO named temps (no 'u32 t=a[i]; a[j]=t;'). Named temps force CW's allocator into descending reg assignment; the templess RMW form makes the expression evaluator color base HIGH and walk loads ascending. Cracked gs_gfx fn_800D3190 shuffle block 99.63->100 (base r5/r4->r6, load order 0xc,0x4->0x4,0xc). Overrides kg W1+W2 c_controllable=0 classification for this pattern.

title: #pragma scheduling on
description: Forces CW instruction scheduling to match target order.

title: volatile cast defeats byte/SDA CSE
description: *(volatile u8*)p / *(volatile T*)&lbl forces a reload where CW would CSE two reads.

title: Already byte-exact scratch
description: When a pinned function already checks at 100 percent in the band scratch, run save directly so the win is harvested without speculative edits.

title: Restore dropped float vararg
description: crset cr1eq vs crclr before a varargs call = a dropped FLOAT arg; restore it (use a fr1 param).

title: Negated condition → early return
description: if (cond == X) { call } return 0 generates bne FORWARD. Flip to if (cond != X) return 0; call; to get beq FORWARD (target layout). Key for functions where the equal path jumps forward in target asm.

title: No-residual band save
description: band.py diff/classifier showed no residual; save confirmed an already byte-exact real-C body.

title: Typed prototypes plus nested status guard
description: Replace raw register-call scratch with fixed prototypes and nested byte-status guards so CodeWarrior keeps raw call results in saved regs and emits the target clrlwi/cmplwi branch shape.

title: Pad.c (Dolphin SDK merged TU) needs GC/1.2.5n not 1.3
description: Frankenstein TU mixing PAD/AI/SI/CARD/GX SDK libs; default GC/1.3 mis-set. 1.2.5n regresses 0/14, gains +4 byte-exact. Added override in compile_config.json. Same pattern as SI.c/OSContext.c.

title: Live second callback parameter fixes reg coloring
description: When a reset/start wrapper passes a callback's second argument through unchanged, declare the function with that live parameter and call the callback with it. Keeping r4 live prevents CW from using it as a scratch global-base register, allowing the target r5 global-base coloring.

title: m2c-draft faithful reshape (from-scratch stub)
description: Stub fn at 0%: m2c_draft.py gives near-exact C; transcribe with named locals + correct types, count-var assigned in both branches keeps mtctr+cmpwi guard for for(i=count;i>0;i--). Often 100% first try.

title: Convert rNN-pseudocode trainer stub to real C by mirroring an adjacent matched sibling
description: trainer.c targets are rNN-pseudocode stubs (u32 r3,r4,r27...) measuring ~3%. Convert to real C: get signature from target asm entry (mr. rN,r3 = arg0 check), find an ADJACENT already-matched sibling (e.g. fn_801FA8CC for fn_801FA6D8) that uses the same helpers (fn_801FB1C0 TrainerDataGet(ctx,slot,field,idx), fn_801FAA58 TrainerDataSet(ctx,slot,field,idx,val)) and mirror its nested-call idiom fn_X(fn_801FB1C0(t,0,FIELD,0)). Pass u16 loop index RAW (declare int i; for(i=0;(u16)i<count;i++)) so it emits mr not clrlwi; pass u16 arg WITHOUT explicit &0xFFFF (let u16->u32 promo mask + schedule mr-before-clrlwi). Cracked fn_801FA6D8 68%->100. WALL: classify-loop fns (fn_801F81F8/93F8/87CC) hit loop2 j/item live-range-split reg swap (decl-order INVARIANT, ~94 ceiling).

title: Stub to table lookup
description: Replace raw-register stub with named-local EOF guard plus byte-indexed rodata lookup when target is cmpwi/branch/lbzx.

title: stw @l(r5) vs addi+stw 0(r5) for offset-0 global store
description: CW1.3 -O4p folds @l into stw displacement for isolated single offset-0 global accesses; target uses separate addi+stw. Not steerable from C regardless of struct/array/named-ptr/scheduling. Only occurs for offset 0 where @l can fold; offset-4+ always uses addi form.

title: case 0 + default for dead cmpwi
description: Adding case 0: sharing default body emits target's dead cmpwi r0,0 prologue.

title: Advance dead base pointer
description: When the original array base is dead, reassign the base pointer to the selected element before a field load. This can make CW emit mulli r0; add r3,base,r0; lwz field(r3) instead of addi fieldOffset; lwzx. Cracked gs_npc_interact fn_80010588 with a nested call to keep the color result in r3->r5.

title: Block-scoped local declaration order
description: Use a small case/block with named locals declared in target register order to steer CW register coloring without disturbing surrounding matching code.

title: Block-scope global-pointer locals to force volatile rematerialization
description: When target rematerializes &global into volatile r3 at every use (lis+addi, reused only within call-free regions, NOT held in a saved reg), declare the pointer as SEPARATE block-local vars each with a short call-free live range (one per use site), NOT one function-scope local. A single fn-scope pointer local forces CW to keep it in a nonvolatile (extra stw + whole reg map shifts ~14% off). Separate short-LR block-locals make CW allocate volatile r3 and rematerialize between ranges, matching. Seen in pl_ui_core fn_800676EC (lbl_803A9F08): 83%->97%.

title: Chained init t=tStart=const breaks copy-coalesce reg swap
description: When 'tStart = t;' copies a just-loaded float and CW swaps the two nonvolatile regs (loaded var lands high, copy low; target wants opposite), write 'tStart = t = lbl_X;' as a single chained assignment. CW then loads the constant into the mutated var's (lower) register and fmr-copies up to the read-only var, matching target. Fixed fn_8010FDF8 t/tStart f28/f31 swap, +0.13%.

title: Combined mismatch fallthrough return
description: For search loops where CW fuses a second positive return into beqlr, combine subtype guards as one negative mismatch condition that continues, then return on fallthrough to emit bne skip; blr.

title: Inline CTR count into for-init
description: for(i=*(u32*)lbl;i!=0;i--) keeps mtctr/bdnz; a separate local + if-guard suppresses it.

title: Declaration-order loop index rotation
description: For nested loops with correct instructions but rotated nonvolatile registers, reorder named loop-index declarations to match the target r31-down map.

title: Dense case set reproduces dead cmpwi range-bound (no beq)
description: A target dead 'cmpwi r,N; b default' (NO beq) at a switch hi-node is CW's dense range check 'cmpwi (maxcase+1); bge default'. Provide DENSE cases {0..N-1} (max=N-1) so CW emits 'cmpwi N' as a range bound with NO conditional branch, matching the dead compare. Sparse cases {..,N} instead produce a binary-tree PIVOT 'cmpwi N' WITH a beq that does not fold. Pattern observed: {0,1,2,3}->cmpwi4, {0,1,2,3,4}->cmpwi5, {0,1,2,3,4,5}->cmpwi6 (all = max+1). pl_ui_core fn_800676EC: 99.99%->100%.

title: Make a load depend on a just-computed pointer to force the addi before the load (scheduling)
description: When CW's scheduler reorders an independent (pointer assign) AFTER a nearby load but target does addi-first: rewrite the load to go THROUGH the computed pointer. E.g. sel=base+0xd40; if(*(s32*)(base+0xdd4)==1) → change to *(s32*)(sel+0x94) (same addr, 0xdd4-0xd40=0x94) so the load data-depends on sel → addi MUST emit first. Cracked battle_waza fn_801DF160 92.86→100. scheduling-off REGRESSES (85). General scheduling lever for addi/load ordering.

title: Direct-call named reshape
description: Replace raw-register/cast-fnptr imported calls with named locals and direct K&R extern calls to restore direct bl and argument order.

title: Doubly linked-list unlink reshape
description: When target writes a node state, compares against SDA head/tail pointers, and updates +0x38/+0x3C links, replace probe/stub C with a typed doubly linked-list unlink using named next/prev fields.

title: Explicit empty switch cases widen range lowering
description: For sparse byte switches, adding empty low cases can force CW to emit the target low-first range tree instead of a descending compare chain; combine with named temps/declaration order when needed.

title: Fix wrong header forward-decl signature to match the real float/pointer args
description: battle_grid fn_801C29C4 stub sig (s32,s32) conflicted with the real (void* obj, f32 value) — the float arg is in f1 not r4 so the s32 sig cannot match. The forward decl in include/game/battle/battle.h had the stale guess; update it to the real sig. Body: set float@4, RMW field0 (clear 0x40000000 via rlwinm, set 0x08000000 via oris), call HSD_FObjReqAnimAll(obj->0x14). 6 to 100.

title: FPR f1/f2 load-order scheduling at -O4
description: At -O4,p, CW scheduling reorders independent float loads: for 'vec[0]=A; vec[1]=B;' before a call where f2=B is the stable call arg, CW loads B(f2) FIRST, A(f1) second. Target has source order (A first). Scheduling-off causes 40-50% regression at -O4. Not fixable with any pragma. WALL when this is the dominant residual.

title: goto flips beq branch target block
description: if (== 0x2a) goto fn_call; return 0; fn_call: return fn(); puts fn() as beq target (after li r3,0 fall-through), matching CW block layout where equal path jumps forward to the call block

title: Goto-shaped flag materialization
description: Use explicit goto success/failure labels when CW must emit li 1; b end; li 0 for a synthesized boolean flag, avoiding an extra branch from else-if lowering. Cracked gs_npc_interact fn_8000FE38.

title: goto-to-one-label for duplicated flag-set blocks; OR-condition inlines the shared alt block
description: Multi-condition flag dispatch where several conditions set ok=1: CW duplicates the li r0,1 block if written as nested if/else. Use per-condition if(F==1) goto ok1; with ONE ok1: ok=1; label so all beq share it. Group the ok=0 conditions with || in one if so its block inlines mid-function matching target layout. Also hidden 2nd arg via chained call fn_b(fn_a(...), K) and dead cmpwi via if(result==-1) return 1; before return 1. Cracked fn_8025BFAC 32 to 100.

title: goto single-exit keeps 0/1 return flag in non-volatile (defeats per-branch const-fold)
description: asm returns a 0/1 flag via ONE shared 'mr r3,rNV' epilogue: flag set once to 1 at top, conditionally to 0 in fail paths, keep-paths branch to epilogue with flag intact. Per-branch 'return result' lets CW const-fold each exit to 'li r3,const'. Route EVERY exit through 'goto done; ... done: return result;' so the flag lives in a non-volatile (r31) and all paths merge to a single 'mr r3,r31' epilogue.

title: Inline size expr (no named local) to flip implicit-temp vs named-local coloring tie
description: When tableSize=param*sizeof is a NAMED local, CW pins it to the higher saved reg (r25) and the masked clrlwi temp (defined first by data dependence) to the lower (r23); target wants the reverse. Decl-order is INVARIANT (every effectCount/tableSize permutation=99.74; naming masked u32 regresses to 50, u16 to 99.74). FIX: inline the (int)param*sizeof(...) expression directly in BOTH the alloc call arg and memset (delete the named local) so both values are implicit temps; the product temp then claims r23 and masked floats to r25 - byte-exact. Cracked gs_effect fn_80130CE0 99.74->100.

title: Local u16 argument prototype
description: For call-setup order residuals around a casted argument, declare the callee's parameter as u16 locally so CodeWarrior owns the zero-extension at the call boundary.

title: Local u32 prototype with u16 cast
description: For call-setup order residuals where CodeWarrior emits mr r3 before clrlwi r4, widen the local callee prototype to u32 for the argument and keep an explicit u16 cast at the call site to force clrlwi before first-arg setup.

title: M2C shape with explicit byte/halfword casts
description: For SHAPE residuals from m2c drafts, use named locals plus explicit (u8) call-result compares and (u16) low-half casts to force clrlwi before cmplwi/extsh.

title: Mask expression steers argument setup order
description: Replacing a u16 cast argument with an equivalent '& 0xFFFF' expression can make CodeWarrior schedule a neighboring argument move before the clrlwi mask, fixing byte-order residuals without changing value semantics.

title: MMIO source correction
description: When a target leaf accessor starts from lis 0xCC00 and reads a hardware register, replace an inferred cached array/global lookup with a volatile MMIO read at the target offset.

title: Nested call result argument
description: Pass a call result directly as the later argument to avoid a temporary r0 move and preserve the target volatile-register handoff.

title: Write !(x==K) instead of x!=K to reproduce early-lmw epilogue scheduling
description: When a function ends return (u16)f(...) != K and target schedules the lmw saved-reg restore BEFORE the != bool idiom subfic/subic/subfe, write return !((u16)f(...) == K) instead. The == form lets CW interleave the lmw earlier. Cracked colosseum_battle fn_80256A68 93.33 to 100. The != and split-temp forms regressed.

title: Peephole off preserves separate compare
description: Use #pragma peephole off around short validity-check functions when CodeWarrior collapses mr+cmpwi into mr.; this preserved the target mr/cmpwi/li branch shape in pl_menu_middle::fn_8006B4AC.

title: Pragmas split byte compare and stabilize call setup
description: For small non-leaf wrappers, #pragma scheduling off keeps LR-save/argument-load order target-like, and #pragma peephole off can prevent clrlwi.+branch fusion so CW emits clrlwi; cmplwi; beq.

title: Redundant u16/u8 cast forces mr round-trip
description: Casting (u16)/(u8) on a call whose return is ALREADY that type forces CW to clrlwi the result into a scratch (r0) then mr into the surviving non-volatile. Drop the redundant cast so the mask lands directly in the home register (clrlwi r31,r3,16) and the compare reads it directly.

title: extern scalar + &lbl for SDA array
description: extern u16 lbl_X; (&lbl_X)[idx] emits li rN,lbl@sda21 + offset; array decl emits wrong lis+addi.

title: Selected stack entry getter
description: Return *(u32*)(stack_base + (*(u32*)(stack_base+index_offset) << entry_shift)); simple selected-entry table getter.

title: Sibling-pattern named shape rewrite
description: Replace raw-register imported body with named C copied from an adjacent same-shape sibling, preserving call order and return tests.

title: Single trailing return funnels result to r31
description: When result var is constant 0 in one branch, CW steals its r31 for a sibling pointer. Route all result-returns to ONE trailing 'return result' (no in-branch return result) so result stays live across branches and pins r31; per-fn opt_level 2 needed for var-index base+const-disp lwz (O4 reassociates to lwzx).

title: Frameless copy-loop: 3x Vec3f struct-copy + re-read loop count + declare counter first
description: gs_colsys CD6C copy-loop (src 0x40 stride -> dest 0x28, copy 0x24=3 Vec3f + zero +0x24, over wzx->count): cracked 2.6->100. (1) copy as THREE ((Vec3f*)dst)[k]=((Vec3f*)src)[k] (each 0xC -> CW pair+single 2L2S; one 0x24 block-copy gives wrong grouping pair-pair-pair; 9 explicit word-copies at -O4 -> mtctr block loop=0%); (2) RE-READ wzx[1] in the loop condition (don't cache triCount) -> keeps wzx live (lwzu) + matches per-iter count reload AND prevents mtctr; (3) declare loop counter i BEFORE srcTri/dstEntry so it claims the LOW volatile reg r6 (decl-order DOES reach volatile regs for loop vars). COL_WZX=COL_STATE->wzxDataPtr emits lwzu.

title: stw combined vs materialized global struct store
description: At -O4,p, CW peephole merges 'addi r5,r5,lbl@l + stw r3,0(r5)' into 'stw r3,lbl@l(r5)'. Target sometimes has the split form. Scheduling-off/peephole-off both cause severe score regression (~20-50% drop) at -O4. Not C-steerable without frame/register changes. WALL on sight when combined form is the only diff.

title: One-case switch forces beq;b;L shape
description: switch(x){case 0:...;break;} emits beq L; b end; L: form no if/goto reproduces.

title: Switch-shaped equality branch
description: For tiny shape residuals where CodeWarrior inverts an equality check and removes the unconditional branch, use a switch on the loaded status with a -1 case and default goto to preserve beq plus b layout.

title: Flip guard to positive-return so the sentinel return shares the tail (code-layout)
description: When target shares a tail (reached by beq, placed AFTER the action block) but CW inlines it before via bne: flip → . The action becomes the inline then-block, return-0 the shared tail. Cracked battle_waza fn_801DA74C 82.92→100. (Does NOT help loop-body ifs with no return tail, e.g. fn_801DB8FC stays 96.86 branch-layout wall.)

title: u16 loop bound local
description: For small loops, type the loop bound local as u16 after removing raw rNN locals; this can shorten live-range pressure and free the target nonvolatile register map.

title: u8* base ptr fixes ptr+idx add operand order
description: At O4, void*+(u8*)cast base in *(base+idx+disp) emits add rDst,rIdx,rBase (idx first); typing the local as u8* from the start makes base the dominant operand -> add rDst,rBase,rIdx matching target. Cracked fn_801D1D58 99.84->100 (commutative add operand swap, the only residual after peephole-off).

title: Volatile flag reload plus fallthrough branch shape
description: Use volatile byte reads for repeated packed flag tests and write the zero/end case as the non-fallthrough branch so CW reloads the flag byte and emits the target bne/fallthrough loop shape.

title: top-tested while(count>0) + count-- last yields mtctr/bdnz; goto past post-loop li-0 for found-skip
description: Find-first-zero loop: write while(count>0){if(*p==0){result=*(p+1);goto have;} p+=8; count--;} result=0; have: . Top-test + count-- as last stmt makes CW use CTR (mtctr/bdnz) not subic./bne. do-while(--count!=0) or mid-loop counter-exit gives subic. Put li r4,0 AFTER loop (shared exhausted/count<=0) and skip it via goto on found.

title: Hoist a repeated symbol base into a local pointer for saved-reg reuse + lhzx
description: When a function uses the same extern array/symbol base (e.g. lbl_80268940) at multiple offsets across loops, target hoists &symbol into ONE saved reg and reuses (addi base,off; lhzx). Recomputing lis/addi inline (CW default) shifts ALL register numbers (extra saved reg) -> huge reg-coloring cascade. FIX: u8* tbl = symbol; use tbl+off everywhere. Got fn_80076054 86->93 + fixed lhzx indexed-load. Pair with peephole off.

title: Defer cached base ptrs past pre-loop call; assign-order picks r31
description: Big loop fn caching struct-base S + table-base tbl in non-vol regs: decl-initializing a base (pre-bl) makes CW duplicate-materialize it (extra saved reg, stmw r22/r23 vs r24). Fix: declare both UNINITIALIZED, assign AFTER the pre-loop bl; later-assigned base gets higher reg (assign tbl then S -> S=r31). Collapses to 8 regs (stmw r24).

title: Bench target object pairs by symbols.build.txt name (structural renames)
description: auto_01_800055E0_text.o pairs symbols by NAME; structurally-XD-renamed fns (symbols.build.txt) are absent under fn_ name -> band shows 0.0 even for asm wrapper. Rename scratch fn to the symbols.build.txt name (e.g. fn_8005DC24 -> dbgMenuFieldCameraChangeDisp) to measure. Cracked fn_8005DC24 100%.

title: ((u32)*p>>N)&1 over (s8)*p<0
description: Bit-shift form emits extrwi+cmplwi matching target instead of extsb+cmpwi.

title: Block-scope decl forces SDA reload
description: Separate { } blocks defeat CSE of a twice-loaded SDA symbol; opt-level gated.

title: Branch-fusion levers need a statement-block case body
description: switch-one-case-beq-b and ctr-loop-inline crack the bne-tail/b-found vs fused-beq shape ONLY when the case/transform body is a real statement block. Inert when the body is a bare in-loop goto (the goto collapses pre-codegen). Round-2 gs_render: fn_800DCD98 cracked (stmt block), fn_800DAF60 NOT (in-loop goto).

title: Cache repeated struct-array float reads into named f32 locals to match target FPR retention
description: When target keeps proj_a[0]/proj_a[1] alive in FPRs across multiple compare blocks while RELOADING the other operand (proj_b) per use, cache ONLY the retained side into named f32 locals (ax=proj_a[0]; ay=proj_a[1]) assigned LAZILY right before first use; read the reloaded side directly. Lifted fn_80191118 98.33->99.48 (>prior 99.44 wall). Residual = 1-reg FPR permutation (ay lands f4 vs target f3); decl-order swap and eager-hoist do NOT move it (opt4 colors by live-range).

title: case2/3 mirror block-temps via [w,i,n] decl + count pins r31
description: When two structurally-identical switch cases need MIRRORED non-volatile temp assignment (case2 w=r27/n=r25, case3 w=r25/n=r27), declare block temps with the index in the MIDDLE (e.g. [w,i,n]): the middle decl takes r26 in both cases, while the 1st/3rd decls swap positionally between cases. Also: the first call-defined function-scope survivor (e.g. count=fn()) is PINNED to r31 invariant to decl order; the other survivors fill r30/r29/.. in decl order, so you cannot place a later pointer in r31 via decl order alone. Seen on fn_8004C120 pl_poke_detail: 90.86->99.32, residual=count<->ptr r31/r28 permutation.

title: j=i const-folds to li when i is provably 0 (target keeps mr)
description: Two parallel loop counters init i=0; j=i: CW const-propagates i=0 into j -> li rN,0, but target emits mr rN,rM (register copy). Reordering inits/volatile-store separation does not defeat the fold. Plus div-by-const magic schedule interleave + 2nd-loop or-result allocator reg preference. Confirmed gs_field_colquery fn_8011207C 96.74 (>12 prior attempts). WALL.

title: int->float magic const pools anonymous (@nnn) in isolated band TU vs program-shared named lbl
description: CW emits the 0x4330000080000000 int->float magic as an anonymous pooled @nnnn sdata2 const when a TU is compiled in isolation (band), but the real link shares ONE named symbol (e.g. lbl_8047BF80). Anon-vs-named reloc caps <100. Not C-source steerable. WALL class. Seen: ui_core fn_80061240 88.71.

title: bitmap bit-clear tail: byte_index lands r4 not r0 at O4 -> addi+lbzx vs add+lbz; O2 fixes tail but regresses head
description: fn_801D1D58 96.85 WALL. Real-C bitmap register+clear. Tail bit-clear *(obj+arg0/8+0x402)&=~mask: target keeps byte_index in r0 (cannot addi r0,r0,imm) -> add r3,r3,r0; lbz 0x402(r3). At O4 CW colors byte_index to r4 -> addi r4,r4,0x402; lbzx. s32-mask+&0xFF fixes the mr round-trip. block-scope p AND struct-field-0x402 both still fold to lbzx at O4. O2 fixes tail (D1B78 lever) but breaks head (arg0->r31, extra mr r6, extra clrlwi). peephole-off/decl-swap inert. SHAPE verdict, opt-coupled.

title: fn_801D2080 98.22 classifier says REG-COLORING but it is a SCHEDULING wall (audioHandle mr round-trip via r0)
description: 2-line residual: bl fn_8016557C; target mr r30,r3 direct; ours mr r0,r3 ... mr r30,r0 (scheduler hoists lis r3,0x1 for fn_800F9418 first arg into post-call slot, parks audioHandle in r0). Non-volatile coloring ALREADY matches target (wazaHandle r31, audioHandle r30) so decl-order is inert. Proof it is scheduling not coloring: scheduling-off fixes the round-trip but breaks epilogue store order (li r4 vs li r0 + lwz restore reorder) = opposing constraints; decl-swap regresses to 10 lines. classify_residual REG-COLORING is a false-positive (counts register-differing lines). Confirmed WALL, registered EQUIVALENT.

title: Target retains a dead cmplw our CW's DCE elides; no control-flow form or pragma preserves it
Dead bool-materialize compare
description: if(x==0)return 0; if(x==-1)return 1; return 1; cracks some bool-materialize walls.

title: disable old m2c body via #if 0 + rename _dead
description: To swap a band rNN draft for real C without matching the whole old body in an Edit: flip the asm #if 1->#if 0, write new fn in the #else, then open '#if 0\nvoid fn_X_dead(void){' right before the old decls so the old body+its #endif become a skipped dead function.

title: do/while reproduces CW redundant induction counter
description: Rewriting a fixed-trip for(i=0;i<N;i++) as i=0;do{...;i++}while(i<N) can recover CW's redundant induction-variable + entry-pointer non-vol reuse (fn_801054B8 81->85). Does NOT fix volatile-reg coloring of loop-carried base ptrs (r3-5 vs target r10-12).

title: do{...break}while(0) + compound-assign
description: Reaches lwzu/update-form via do/while(0) with break and p+=K compound assignment.

title: Restore dropped leading int vararg
description: mr rN,r3 before a varargs call = a dropped leading int arg; restore it.

title: f32 buf[N] to force frame size
description: Convert scalar f32 locals to an array to reserve the target's larger stack frame.

title: #pragma fp_contract on
description: Fuses a*b+c into fmadds (sticky pragma).

title: int-from-float via (s32)f reads fctiwz low-word
description: m2c renders (s32)floatval's stfd/lwz-0xc as a bogus '+ &lbl' add; the real C is just w + (s32)colf. Drop the pointer-add. Also fn arg list often has fewer args than m2c shows (no float vararg).

title: Frameless leaf accessor r3-recycle is a volatile-register WALL (decl-order cannot reach)
description: Tiny gs_colsys accessors u16/u8/u32 f(u16 idx){if(idx>=count)return 0; return table[idx].field;} compile frameless (no stwu/saved regs). Residual r3-vs-r4: target preserves param r3 and uses r4/r5 for base-lis/mulli; CW GC/1.3 opt_level 1 recycles dead r3->base. The tie is in VOLATILE regs (r0-r5) so the decl-order/named-locals lever (which only reorders SAVED-reg r14-r31 coloring) literally cannot apply - no saved regs exist. Tried off/idx/result/base/e named locals, array-idx, ternary, single-return, two-ptr, opt0-4, peephole/sched off: all <=97.69. classify says REG-COLORING exit0 but its heuristic assumes saved-reg coloring. permuter-NOWIN. WALL.

title: post-call frsp-dest f1-vs-f2 FPR coloring (WALL)
description: fn_80194510/5B0 hsd_cobj: opt4+temp reaches 99.5%; residual=target keeps tan in f1 (frsp in-place), CW binds [0x44]->f1 & evacuates tan to f2. Unbreakable: 12 manual source forms + permuter 500k iters (best score 80->50, --stop-on-zero never fired).

title: full prototypes on extern calls fix arg scheduling
description: Ghidra drafts declare callees with empty params (extern u32 fn();). CW then can't schedule the call's constant/arg setup well, leaving li/clrlwi at the call site instead of hoisted into the prologue. Replacing with full typed prototypes (extern u32 fn(void*, u32, u16, u32);) lets CW schedule + type-mask correctly. Took colosseum_event fn_802086E8 84.62->92.31%. Pair with the void-pseudo-register-param signature fix (give Ghidra 'u32 r3; u32 r4;' uninit locals a real (void* r3, u32 r4) signature). Residual walls for this file remain: prologue mr-order, single constant-hoist (li r6,0), mr.-fusion, carry-trick reg (3-bool), param-reg-alloc.

title: fix Ghidra array sizes + reverse decl order for stack layout
description: Ghidra drafts oversize stack arrays (e.g. u16 keys[0x100] when the real frame shows ~0x14 entries) — read the target's stack offsets to size them, halving the frame. CW lays locals out in REVERSE declaration order (last declared = lowest addr), so to match target's low-to-high layout, declare arrays in reverse. Also: pass a caller's output-buffer param (arg3) to the inner call rather than a bogus local; replace manual N-wide loop unrolls with a simple for-loop (CW auto-unrolls to the matching mtctr/bdnz); widen an inner call's u16 key param to u32 to pass it raw (mr) instead of masked (clrlwi); unify multiple 'return 0' via a shared goto label; a redundant double if(x==0) check can match a target's duplicate beq. Took gs_field_world fn_8012805C 68->97.57%.

title: goto _ret0 flips branch inversion
description: if(ptr==NULL) goto _ret0; flips CW's bne load-path to target's beq null-path for getters.

title: goto-shared-call-idiom
description: Mirror CW's shared-call jumptable-free dispatch ('bne CALL...CALL: X(r3)') with explicit C goto when a value can come from two sources (param or fallback) into one call; pair with unconditional-init/conditional-override (v=param; if(v==NULL)v=E24()) so CW emits 'mr r3,rN; bne CALL'. Took fn_80129094 49.82->99.11%.

title: goto shared exit label forces CW tail-merge of identical return-0 blocks
description: When many 'return K' paths share a common value (e.g. return 0 -> B070), CW under peephole-off emits a local 'li r3,0;b' per occurrence instead of branching to one shared block. Converting bare 'return 0;' to 'goto ret0;' with one 'ret0: return 0;' at function end reproduces the target's single shared exit block (b SHARED). Keep genuinely-local returns (those that go to their own li r3,0;b, not the shared block) as plain 'return 0;'. Cracked fn_8007AB10 menu_exdisc2 93->98.7%.

title: 4-vs-5 nonvol GPR frame-size cascade (dead param pinned)
description: Target keeps a now-dead param in a nonvol (5 GPRs, larger frame) so an IV claims its own register; faithful C lets CW reclaim the dead param's reg (4 GPRs, smaller frame), shifting every stfd/psq/stmw save offset -> huge line cascade at lowish %. Forcing CW to waste a register on a dead local is not reachable from faithful C. Confirmed gs_field_colquery fn_8011163C 84.35. WALL.

title: loop-counter<->pointer pinned-r31 swap (NOT C-steerable)
description: When a long-lived pointer (defined first, NULL-checked immediately) and the loop counter both survive the whole loop, CW glues the pointer to r31 and the counter to a lower nonvol. Target may want the reverse. Decl-order, i=0 hoist, and comma-for-increment all fail to move it (confirmed gs_colsys fn_8010D8D4 + sibling fn_8010D20C, 5+ orderings each). Compiler allocator artifact -> WALL.

title: Implicit (float)->u8 store: omit (s32) cast to drop clrlwi
description: When storing a float expr to a u8 lvalue (e.g. color.a = floatexpr*scale), write it WITHOUT an explicit (s32) cast. CW emits fctiwz->stfd->lwz->stb directly. Adding (s32) inserts a redundant clrlwi r,r,24 mask before stb that target lacks. ui_core fn_80063AD4: dropping the cast removed 3 clrlwi and jumped 97.83->99.93%.

title: inline repeated call to defeat CSE
description: When the target re-calls a getter (e.g. fn_80119ED0(x)) for each comparison in a ||/if chain, do NOT store it in a temp — CW does not CSE function calls, so inlining fn(x)==A || fn(x)==B emits N separate bl's matching the target. Also: Ghidra imports that declare uninitialized 'u32 r3; u32 r4;' locals as stand-in params must be given a real signature (void* a, void* b, ...) so params save to r28-r31. Took colosseum_event fn_802019BC 78.80->95.43% (then 99.57% with #pragma scheduling off to stop result-store/next-arg interleaving).

title: Inner parens flip FP add order
description: Parenthesizing (a±b) reorders CW's FP add evaluation to match target.

title: int->float plain-cast vs double-word idiom: @nnn bias wall
description: gs_field_world float fns: the #else drafts replaced CW int->float double-word idioms with plain (f32)(s32)x casts ('x86-correct' per code comment). The cast generates the SAME idiom (extsb/xoris/stw/lfd/fsubs) but with an ANONYMOUS @nnn bias pool constant; target uses the NAMED lbl_8047D008 (signed bias 0x4330000080000000) / lbl_8047D010 (unsigned 0x4330000000000000). Rewriting with the explicit idiom (cvt[1]=v^0x80000000; cvt[0]=0x43300000; f=*(f64*)cvt-lbl_8047D008;) fixes the symbol but introduces fsub+frsp (vs target fsubs) + reg-swaps — net WORSE than the plain cast's single @nnn diff. So @nnn conversion-literal is a practical wall. Real wins on fn_8011F910 (75.73->86.99%): retype getter s32->s8 (target extsb before convert), pass id raw (drop (u16), widen param to u32), switch->if/else-if chain (sequential cmplwi vs binary-search dispatch).

title: auto-jumptable lands at wrong rodata addr when fn was previously asm-wrapper (WALL)
description: objdiff resolves switch @ha/@l to an ADDRESS then symbolmap-lookups the name (proven: 6 sibling jumptable fns match at 100%). But when you write REAL C for a switch fn that was previously an asm-wrapper, CW emits a NEW auto-jumptable that lands at a DIFFERENT rodata address than the target's symbolmap addr (e.g. fn_8000F35C: @446 vs jumptable_802E4C80). CW appends the new jt rather than placing it in the target's slot, because the asm-wrapper fns around it don't reproduce their rodata in isolation. Unfixable for a single function -- needs TU-wide roda alignment. Confirmed fn_8000F35C: peephole off cracked ALL code (92.3%->99.76%), ONLY the jt @ha/@l remained.

title: Late local assignment controls value-materialize emission order
description: At opt0, assigning a constant local (e.g. u32 size=0x40) at its point-of-use rather than top-of-function moves the li/materialize down to match target emission order + frees the register-allocation order for downstream call args. Cracked fn_8017DF4C (opt0 + late size assign) 90->100%.

title: unrolled ptr-search: lwzu-walk needs loop form but reallocs params
description: fn_800E9148 gs_material: hand-unrolled 6-elem ptr search (base+0,0x58,0xb0,0x108,0x160,0x1b8 stride 0x58, early-exit). Target reads elem0/1 base-relative off r29 then lwzu-walks r4 for elem2-4 + plain lwz elem5. else-if chain w/ (p+=0x58) in cond = good param coloring (entry r31) BUT O4 strength-reduces p back to base+const (lwz 0xb0(r3), no lwzu) -> 91.80 SHAPE. for(j=0;j<6;j++){if(*p==entry)break;p+=0x58} fully unrolls AND yields lwzu BUT walker takes a nonvolatile + params shift 3 regs down (entry r31->r28) -> 88%. Tension unresolved in ~4 attempts. WALL 91.80.

title: m2c_draft + clean rewrite of pseudo-register Ghidra drafts
description: trainer.c type-dispatch fns are raw pseudo-register placeholders (u32 r0,r3..r31 locals, ((void(*)())fn)() indirect calls, wrong void signature) at 6-37%. Fix: run m2c_draft.py for a clean readable draft, then rewrite as idiomatic C with the REAL signature (the m2c 'unset r3' = the param), inline-call-no-CSE for re-called getters, and shared-call (compute arg into a temp then ONE call, not duplicated per if/else branch). Also keys[(u8)i] (recompute offset, target clrlslwi) not keys[i] (incremental counter). Took fn_80200E00 37.35->97.92%. Residual = reg-alloc coloring permutation (2-swap of arg/t and keysbase/i registers).

title: Convert if-else-if dispatch chains to switch() to match target BST codegen
description: In menu state-machine TUs (menu_tool2.c), target compiles if(x==A)..else if(x==B).. chains as balanced-BST switches (cmpwi pivot; beq; bge tree). Rewriting the if-else-if as switch(x){case A:..case B:..default:} matches. ALSO: add the target's dead-leaf cases (values whose body==default) as explicit empty/fallthrough cases so CW builds the full BST with dead cmpwi leaves. Got fn_80076054 dispatch 75->86, moveId nested switch. CAVEAT: GC/1.3 still RANGE-COLLAPSES contiguous dead leaves (cmpwi 0xb4->bge 0xb5) where the target build kept explicit beq leaves - that residual is a CW-version WALL.

title: Named-struct cast for block copy
description: *(struct S*)dst=*(struct S*)src matches CW lwzu/stwu/bdnz interleave; anon casts fail.

title: Nest call into arg to keep r3
description: Inlining a call into the next call's arg avoids a non-volatile spill (smaller prologue).

title: -O4,s TU flag
description: Size-opt flag; correct for some TUs (script/event), net-negative for others (battle).

title: opt_level 2 trims O4 strength-reduction noise on mixed base/walk-ptr search
description: fn_800E9148 gs_material: #pragma optimization_level 2 (vs TU -O4) lifts 91.80->92.99 by reducing strength-reduction artifacts, but does NOT restore the lwzu walk (still lwz base+const). opt 1=88.86, opt 3/4=91.80. Core lwzu-walk-vs-param-coloring SHAPE wall persists at all opt levels.

title: #pragma optimize_for_size on (per-FUNCTION)
description: Per-fn pragma (NOT the -O4,s TU flag) forces stmw/lmw block-save + real divwu (defeats reciprocal-mult). band save DROPS surrounding pragmas -> must live in canon before band_integrate. Cracked 4/6 pl_battle_logic.

title: byte | -0x100 li/or-vs-oris/ori is context-dependent WALL
description: (s32)ctx[0x8b]|-0x100 compiles to li r0,-0x100;or in matched scene_init fn_8003B6D0 but oris/ori (immediate) in ui_core fn_800615F4/fn_80068BB0 with IDENTICAL expr+flags(-O4,p). Materialization depends on surrounding register liveness, NOT C-steerable: tested literal 0xFFFFFF00u, (s32) casts, u8* ctx[] indexing, named s32 var, peephole on/off, opt_level 0-4. Caps these fns ~95% near-100. WALL on sight at byte|0xFFFFFF00 arg sites in ui_core.

title: Param-area reservation differs cross-build (frame 0x28 vs 0x20); unreachable from C, same family as lr-before-stwu prologue
peephole off emits clrlwi+cmplwi instead of record-form clrlwi. (and forces arg-save-before-check)
description: With peephole on, MWCC fuses (u16)x!=0 to record-form 'clrlwi. rN,rM,16' and reads the arg reg directly, coalescing away the save. With #pragma peephole off it emits 'clrlwi r3,r30,16; cmplwi r3,0' from the saved local, and saves all args (mr r27..r31) before the check. Helped fn_801040F0 64->91.79 (residual r30/r31 key/data coalesce wall) and fn_80103EF4 stbu->lis+addi.

title: peephole off cracks switch+compare-fusion codegen
description: For functions where CW fuses a mask+compare into record-form (clrlwi.) or reorders flag-init (li rN) vs target's separate clrlwi+cmplwi, #pragma peephole off reproduces the target's non-fused instruction order. Confirmed fn_8000F35C (gs_npc_interact): 92.32%->99.76% with peephole off (fixed clrlwi record-form + li r31 scheduling). Only residual was a jumptable roda-positioning reloc artifact (auto-jt not at symbolmap addr when fn was previously asm-wrapper).

title: Pointer-walk loop makes CW unroll find-loop 9x (ctr=8)
description: For an N-entry {u16 key;u16 val} table search, writing a pointer-walk loop (u16* p=&tbl; for(i=0;i<N;i++){if(*p==key){result=p[1];break;} p+=2;}) makes CW 1.3 fully unroll the inner compares (9-wide, ctr=N/9) matching the target's unrolled SHAPE. Indexed access tbl[i*2] stays ROLLED with slwi. Pair with switch(a){case0:case1:..;case2:default:break;} for the beq;bge split range-check shape. Got fn_80068BB0 0->90%.

title: hsd_pobj_disp tail SList-builders: pool-base LICM hoist wall
description: fn_801ADF54/ADD48/ADE50 (after random.c in TU, NOT in melee pobj.c=NOT-FARMABLE). Reconstructed faithful C from base.o asm: SList of {next@0,data@4} from desc[] 8-byte array, node->data=desc->0 while desc->4!=0, alloc via HSD_ObjAlloc(&lbl_80465688), assert line 0x485 file=&lbl_8047DCB8 msg=&lbl_8047DCC0. WALL ~48-62%: target hoists lbl_80465688 addr into dedicated nonvol r31 across loop (LICM) + node in r28 + list_p=node via mr (not lwz-reload); CW rematerializes lis/addi per-iter, no 4th-nonvol alloc. opt0/1/2 all <=48 (opt0 worse). NOTE base.o is ground truth; src/hsd/*_fn_*.inc are STALE/wrong. DO NOT re-grind.

title: s32 local + (s16) cast at use site
description: Declare s32, cast (s16) only at the arithmetic use to place extsh where target wants it.

title: (s16)f32 direct cast
description: Direct (s16)f32 (not (s16)(s32)f32) avoids a spurious extsh before sth; pairs w/ peephole off.

title: Scalar pairs (not 2-arrays) defeat LICM hoist of &arr[1]
description: When a fn passes &buf[0]/&buf[1] of a 2-elem array to a callee inside a loop, CW LICM-hoists the base+4 address into a non-volatile (inflates frame + reg count). Replacing each 2-array with two separate scalar vars (u32 a0,a1) makes CW fold the address into the call-site addi, no hoist. gs_material fn_800EA960 91.30->95.88. Stack-slot order: CW assigns scalars HIGH addr to FIRST-declared, so declare hi-word first (c1,c0,b1,b0,a1,a0).

title: scheduling on + peephole off
description: COMBO unlocks load-batching where neither pragma alone matches.

title: int->float magic lfd names private @NNNN, not .sdata2 lbl (WALL)
description: CW int->float/int->double conversion emits lfd of its OWN private constant (@NNNN@sda21) for the 0x4330000080000000 bias. Target references a NAMED .sdata2 symbol (e.g. lbl_8047D0E0@sda21). Pure C cannot force CW to name a user .sdata2 symbol for the bias. Only inline asm (lfd fN, lbl_XXX(r2)) cracks it -- forbidden in REAL-C-only benchmarks. Confirmed on fn_801327E0 (effect_util): fp_contract gets fmadds but bias lfd stays @1866 vs lbl_8047D0E0, caps ~57%. See also dropped-float-vararg/frsp-dest variants.

title: SDA21 float-const reloc baked to literal r2-offset (WALL)
description: pl_ui_core fn_80060A28(93.14)/fn_80068418(97.00): ALL residual = target lfs/lfd lblXXXX@sda21 vs ours -0xNNNN(r2) baked literal. extern u8 + *(f32*)& consts fed into float ARITH/CMP lose the R_PPC_EMB_SDA21 reloc (pyelftools: 0 type-109 in-fn vs sibling fn_80061454 which passes consts to CALLS keeps reloc). INVARIANT to: typed-ptr vs (u32)& cast, *(volatile f32*)&, clean mtctr/bdnz loop reformulation, per-fn #pragma optimization_level 0/1/2/4 (band def-span extract ignores surrounding pragma). RELOC-class wall, classifier mislabels SCHEDULING. Faithful named-local C left in scratch. DO NOT re-grind.

title: Signed int->float via (f32)(s32) cast emits xoris 0x8000 idiom
description: When a u16/u32 value must convert to float with the SIGNED int->float idiom (lis 0x4330; xoris rX,rX,0x8000; ...; fsubs) instead of unsigned (clrlwi rX,16), write (f32)(s32)expr. The (s32) cast signals signed so CW uses xoris 0x8000 with NO extsh (value stays 32-bit). Proven on pl_battle_waza fn_801DF1D0: flipped the conversion from clrlwi(16) to xoris(0x8000), +reg-coloring cascade.

title: Named-struct copy for store order
description: Matches CW contiguous-word store interleave when a plain copy mismatches on order.

title: struct/array member access groups index*stride+field_off before base, vs reassociated byte ptr-arith at O4
description: At -O4 raw (u8*)base + idx*N + K reassociates; &arr[idx] via a sized struct/array emits mulli/slwi idx then +K then +base. Cracked fn_800B5CD8 47->93%.

title: Sub-base pointer DECLARATION order controls CW non-vol reg assignment
description: Multiple derived sub-base pointers (S+0x118, S+0x124, ...) held in non-vol regs: CW assigns them registers in DECLARATION order (1st-declared -> highest r29, descending), NOT assignment/use order. Reorder the local DECLARATIONS to match the target's sub-base->reg permutation. Verified flipping r29/r28/r27/r26 -> target r26/r29/r28/r27.

title: switch case-body source order sets layout
description: For a CW switch whose case bodies are laid out in SOURCE order (not value order), reorder the case labels to match the target's body layout (e.g. 'case 5:' before 'case 6:' when target emits case5 body first). Took fn_80128A64 84->92.58%. Combine with u16-return-retype-u32 to drop acc clrlwi and (s32)-cast to force signed cmpwi on a u8 compare.

title: Reorder switch case statements to match target block-emission order
description: CW emits switch case bodies in SOURCE statement order. When target label addresses show a different case order (e.g. state4/5 before state3), reorder the C case statements to match -> aligns the emitted block layout. Worth large gains on big state machines (+3.24% on fn_80044630).

title: Tail branch-inversion + u32-retyped callee return (raw pass)
description: gs_field_world validate-then-act fns (fn_8011A6D4/A860/ACB4/AE40 sudoku cluster) ended with 'if(p!=NULL) act(p); return K;' which CW compiles as both paths merging into 'li r3,K'. Target instead INVERTS: 'if(p==NULL) return K; return act(p);' so the act() callee's return value flows raw to r3 (no li r3,K reset on the call path). PLUS: the tail-callee (fn_8011A000 u16 / A078 u16 / A048 u8 / A060 u8) must have its local extern retyped to u32 so 'return act(p)' emits NO truncation (clrlwi r3,r3,16/24) -- the real u16/u8 type adds a zero-extend the target lacks (see feedback_u16_return_raw_pass_lever). Took all 4 siblings 95.61 -> 98.74. RESIDUAL WALL (regions 1+2): block-1 'mr r0,r3;mr r3,r31;mr r28,r0' base-save buffers through r0 vs target's direct 'mr r28,r3' (scheduling, scheduling-off breaks prologue); block-2 idx/base saved-reg coloring swap (target idx=r28/base=r29, CW coalesces base=r28 across both blocks). 6 levers tried on residual (separate-vars, hoist-ED0, scheduling-off, inline-arg1, decl-swap, peephole-off) all REGRESS.

title: Demote u16 param to s32
description: Emits clrlslwi on the original param reg instead of an extra clrlwi/mr.

title: Declare extern returning u32 + (u16) cast at use forces caller-side clrlwi
description: When target emits clrlwi r3,r3,16 to narrow a function's return before use but OURS elides it (because the extern was declared returning u16, so CW deems the narrowing redundant vs an immediately-truncating sth), redeclare the extern as returning u32 and add an explicit (u16) cast at the call site: x = field + (u16)fn();. The u32 return + cast materializes clrlwi r3,r3,16. Proven on pl_battle_waza fn_801DF1D0 add site.

title: volatile u32 scratch accessed as two u16 halves forces 4-byte slot spacing
description: When two dead-store scratch u16 locals need to land N bytes apart (e.g. sp+0x8 and sp+0xc) but MWCC packs them 2 apart, declare ONE volatile u32 and store via *((volatile u16*)&s + 2) and *(volatile u16*)&s. Models a 4-byte slot accessed at +0/+4. Use when target sth offsets are 4-apart for two u16. Cracked fn_80103EAC in gs_model.c (84->100).

title: u64 load/AND/store reconstructs two-word flag bitfield
description: When asm loads two adjacent struct words (0x114/0x118 big-endian=hi/lo), tests one with and;and;xor;xor;or. (dead 'and rX,hi,0' + 'xor ...,0'), then stores BOTH words back (one unchanged), the source is a u64 op over *(u64*)(p+off): test '(flags & MASK_in_low_word) == 0' and set 'flags |= MASK'. The 32-bit form FOLDS the dead high-mask/xor (CW knows r6=0); only the u64 lowering keeps them un-folded. Redundant store of the unchanged high word = the u64 store writing both. Took people_field fn_80153FEC 61->97.23%. RESIDUAL WALL: CW won't coalesce the mask-high-zero reg with the call's 4th-arg variable (both =0) -> r5<->r6 swap + one redundant li r6,0; pure reg-coloring, byte-identical instructions otherwise.

title: Declare callee void to free its arg register for in-place arithmetic
description: When the target asm shows a function call (bl fnX) with NO r3 arg-setup before it (arg unobservable) but OURS keeps a local alive across the call by preserving it in r3 (forcing subi r4,r3,... instead of in-place subi r3,r3,...), declare that callee void and drop the arg. CW then treats the value as dead past the conversion and does the arithmetic in-place on r3. Proven on pl_battle_waza fn_801DF1D0: fixed subi/xoris r4->r3.

title: volatile pointer copy defeats CW LICM hoist of loop-invariant global address
description: In a state-machine while-loop where one case heavily uses a global array base (&lbl), CW -O4 hoists the invariant address into a loop-spanning non-volatile reg, inflating non-volatile count (stmw) and shifting the whole GPR map. 'u8 *volatile mv = lbl; u8 *m = mv;' inside the case forces a reload, keeping the base in a case-local volatile reg like the target. Pair with shared-load temps so the buffer-copy and call-arg reads share one set of loads. fn_80078D5C 92->95.7%.
